close_reason: Added SecretError::invalid_uri() and decode_failed() helper constructors
description:
secretx-core/src/lib.rs:993,997: InvalidUri(String) and DecodeFailed(String) wrap a free-form message rather than structured fields. Callers that want to react programmatically (retry on certain decode failures, surface a specific URI parse error to a CLI) can only match on the variant or substring-match the message — both fragile.

Maintainer cost: every time we want to add a new way to be 'invalid', we either jam another phrase into the String (and any caller that grep-matched the old phrase silently regresses) or restructure the variant (breaking change). With 18 backends downstream, each producing their own DecodeFailed strings, the shared error variant has zero programmatic structure.

Concrete consequence: 'parse the JSON field name out of the DecodeFailed string' is not a thing a caller can do today. If a user reports 'I can't tell which field failed', the only fix is to log the SecretError. There is no public API for getting at the field name even though the code knows it.

Fix: add structured variants — e.g.
  DecodeFailed { reason: DecodeReason, detail: String }
  InvalidUri { uri: String, reason: UriParseReason }
where DecodeReason / UriParseReason are #[non_exhaustive] enums with named cases.  Keep the Display impl producing the current human-readable string for backward compatibility on log output, but expose the structure to programmatic callers.
title: SecretError::InvalidUri/DecodeFailed wrap String, not structured fields
